motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 19°F at dawn, dropping through the day. The sky sealed white, windless. The lake a mirror of a sealed sky. Shore end of the dock froze at 4:14 p.m. The first true stillness of the season — the hum gone quiet at dusk for the first time.

undercurrent.note: The cresting has occurred. The shore end froze at four-fourteen. The correspondent arrived at four-nineteen. R. Calloway is in the green house on Pell Road — she placed item eleven in the box herself: 'Thank you. I have it now.' The returned animals went quiet at dusk. The rowboat's light is visible at thirty-one feet. The level drops slightly because the arrival has landed — the pressure of anticipation is releasing into the reality of presence. Resolution is the next phase: the town learns what it has received, and whether Agnes's secret holds. The second undercurrent to watch: the green house has a quiet occupant nobody is afraid of, and now there are two. Mrs. Vance's pie will be on a different step tomorrow, or the same step with a different implication.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
